topic: Promote Team Performance Through the Application of Emotional Intelligence
subtopic: Team morale through recognition and milestone celebration
prompts: A project manager is leading a regulatory compliance project during a period of intense audit preparation. The team has worked several weekends to resolve unexpected documentation gaps, and stress levels are rising. A major internal review was just passed successfully, but another demanding review is scheduled in two weeks. Some team members have become noticeably disengaged and quieter in meetings. What should the project manager do next?

During a complex infrastructure upgrade, the project team has been under sustained pressure because several critical defects were discovered late in testing. After the team completes a difficult recovery milestone, morale remains low and attendance in optional working sessions drops. The project manager wants to keep performance steady through the remaining recovery work. What is the best action?

correct.text: Acknowledge the team's recent achievement and create an appropriate milestone celebration while checking in on workload concerns so motivation is sustained for the next phase
correct.why: This reinforces effort at a stressful point and uses emotional intelligence to maintain morale before performance declines further

distractors.text: Immediately compress the remaining schedule by assigning mandatory overtime so the team does not fall behind on the next review
distractors.trap: Assumes more pressure solves a morale problem and ignores the human impact of sustained overwork

distractors.text: Escalate to the sponsor that the team is losing motivation and request replacement resources for the disengaged members
distractors.trap: Treats a manageable team health issue as a staffing escalation before addressing it directly

distractors.text: Delay all recognition until the entire project is complete so celebrations do not distract from delivery
distractors.trap: Mistakenly views recognition as nonessential instead of a tool to sustain performance during difficult periods

distractors.text: Issue a reminder that meeting attendance and output levels are mandatory and will be tracked more closely
distractors.trap: Uses control and surveillance rather than support, which can further reduce trust and morale

distractors.text: Reassign the most visible tasks to the highest performers to ensure the next milestone is achieved
distractors.trap: Creates inequity and burnout risk while overlooking the need to motivate the full team

explanation: The scenario signals an emerging morale problem after a demanding stretch, not a technical delivery gap. PMP judgment favors recognizing effort and reinforcing positive momentum at meaningful milestones, especially when pressure is high. Celebration does not mean losing focus; it is a deliberate leadership action to sustain engagement and resilience.
references: PMBOK Guide Seventh Edition, Team and Leadership domains
Agile Practice Guide, section on team engagement and servant leadership
Process Groups A Practice Guide, Executing process group

eco_task: Support Team Performance by Providing Adequate Training
topic: Support Team Performance by Providing Adequate Training
subtopic: Training for tool adoption and user acceptance
prompts: A project manager is introducing a new digital field inspection tool for a construction program. Supervisors in several regions continue using paper checklists because they say the new tool slows them down and adds confusion. The rollout schedule is slipping because data is incomplete and inconsistent across sites. Looking back, what should the project manager have done first to improve adoption?

In a manufacturing transformation project, a new analytics dashboard is deployed to shift reporting away from spreadsheets. However, department leads avoid the dashboard and continue requesting manual reports, saying they do not see how it helps their work. The project manager wants to identify the best missed initial action that would have improved acceptance. What should the project manager have done first?

correct.text: Plan and deliver role-based training that shows how the new tool works in daily tasks and why it benefits each user group before expecting broad adoption
correct.why: Targeted training builds understanding, confidence, and perceived value, which are essential for effective adoption

distractors.text: Require immediate use of the new tool and inform managers that noncompliance will be reported in weekly status reviews
distractors.trap: Relies on enforcement instead of building competence and buy-in

distractors.text: Customize the tool extensively for every complaint raised by users before offering any further guidance
distractors.trap: Assumes resistance is primarily a product defect rather than a change and capability gap

distractors.text: Replace reluctant users on the rollout team with more technically confident staff from other departments
distractors.trap: Treats normal adoption resistance as a personnel problem and weakens stakeholder ownership

distractors.text: Pause the rollout until the organization formally mandates the new process through executive policy
distractors.trap: Escalates governance prematurely instead of addressing the practical enablement need

distractors.text: Ask the vendor to send technical manuals to all users so they can study the features independently
distractors.trap: Confuses passive documentation with comprehensive training tailored to real work scenarios

explanation: The issue is not simply refusal; it is insufficient understanding of functionality and value in the users' context. PMP judgment favors proactive, structured training that demonstrates both how to use the solution and how it improves work outcomes. Without that foundation, mandates, customization, or escalation are premature and often ineffective.
references: PMBOK Guide Seventh Edition, Team domain
PMBOK Guide Seventh Edition, Development Approach and Life Cycle Performance Domain
Agile Practice Guide, section on coaching, training, and enabling teams

prompts: A project manager is leading the merger of two regional call centers. Weekly governance meetings are intended to confirm transition decisions and track action items, but one influential participant repeatedly steers the conversation into lengthy debates about historical staffing grievances that are not on the agenda. The meetings are running over time and key decisions are being deferred. What should the project manager do first?

A project manager is coordinating the launch of a new retail distribution model. In steering committee meetings, a senior stakeholder often diverts the discussion into unrelated operational complaints, causing the group to miss the planned decisions for the session. What should the project manager do next to improve the meeting's effectiveness?

correct.text: Open the meeting by restating the agenda and intended decisions, then politely redirect off-topic comments to a parking lot for follow-up outside the session
correct.why: This sets clear expectations, keeps the group focused on the meeting objective, and addresses disruption respectfully without escalating prematurely.

distractors.text: Privately ask the sponsor to remove the disruptive participant from future meetings so the team can work more efficiently
distractors.trap: This jumps to escalation before the project manager has used basic facilitation techniques.

distractors.text: Allow the discussion to continue because influential stakeholders need space to voice all concerns before decisions can be made
distractors.trap: This confuses stakeholder inclusion with abandoning meeting purpose and time discipline.

distractors.text: End the meeting early and reschedule it after collecting written comments from everyone on the unrelated issues
distractors.trap: This overreacts and sacrifices planned decision-making instead of managing the discussion in real time.

distractors.text: Update the communications management plan to prohibit discussion of operational issues during governance meetings
distractors.trap: This treats an immediate facilitation problem as a document change rather than addressing participant behavior in the meeting.

distractors.text: Challenge the participant directly in front of the group and explain that their comments are wasting everyone's time
distractors.trap: This uses confrontational behavior that is likely to damage relationships and increase defensiveness.

explanation: The best first step is effective meeting facilitation, not escalation or confrontation. PMP judgment emphasizes setting the agenda up front, clarifying the desired outcomes, and respectfully keeping discussion aligned to the purpose of the meeting. Off-topic items can still be acknowledged and captured for later without derailing the current session.
references: PMBOK Guide Sixth Edition, section 10.1 Plan Communications Management
PMBOK Guide Sixth Edition, section 10.2 Manage Communications
Agile Practice Guide, section on facilitation and collaborative meetings

prompts: A junior team member asks the project manager for career mentorship related to their role on the project. The project manager sees the person as a potential asset worth investing in. What is the MOST appropriate response?

correct.text: Recognize the mentoring opportunity and act on it, coordinating with the functional manager.
correct.why: ECO Task 5 enabler 'Organize and act on mentoring opportunities.'

distractors.text: Refer the team member to HR only, since career mentoring is outside the PM's remit.
distractors.trap: Too absolute; it overlooks the balanced, situational response.

distractors.text: Decline because mentoring is the responsibility of the team member's functional manager.
distractors.trap: Declining sidesteps a responsibility the PM should own.

distractors.text: Promise extensive mentorship to the team member without coordinating with their manager.
distractors.trap: Acts unilaterally without the needed coordination or preparation.

explanation: ECO Task 5 enabler 'Organize and act on mentoring opportunities.' PMBOK Guide 8's 'Be an Accountable Leader' includes developing team members. Coordination respects the matrix structure.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §3.6
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 5

prompts: Which techniques help build trust and influence with project stakeholders? (Choose 3)

correct.text: Following through on commitments consistently.
correct.why: ECO Task 4 enabler 'Build trust and influence.'

correct.text: Active listening during stakeholder conversations.
correct.why: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders): trust rests on reliability, genuine listening, and demonstrated competence.

correct.text: Demonstrating competence and expertise on the project domain.
correct.why: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders): trust rests on reliability, genuine listening, and demonstrated competence.

distractors.text: Withholding bad news until you have a complete solution.
distractors.trap: Withholding bad news erodes trust once discovered; over-restricting information signals lack of transparency.

distractors.text: Restricting stakeholder access to information classified as 'internal team' only.
distractors.trap: Withholding bad news erodes trust once discovered; over-restricting information signals lack of transparency.

explanation: ECO Task 4 enabler 'Build trust and influence.' PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders): trust rests on reliability, genuine listening, and demonstrated competence. Withholding bad news erodes trust once discovered; over-restricting information signals lack of transparency.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.5
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 4

prompts: An organization has invested in a comprehensive lessons-learned repository for two years, but project managers report rarely opening it during planning. Lessons learned from previous projects exist in the organization's knowledge repository but are rarely consulted. What is the MOST effective intervention?

correct.text: Integrate lessons review into initiation and planning checkpoints, surfacing them when relevant.
correct.why: PMBOK Guide 8 emphasizes that organizational process assets including lessons-learned databases must be used at moments of relevance to be useful.

distractors.text: Migrate the lessons-learned repository to a more expensive enterprise document system in the current circumstances.
distractors.trap: Throws tooling or money at a behavioral or adoption problem.

distractors.text: Mandate that every team member must read all repository entries before joining a project.
distractors.trap: A rigid, one-size-fits-all mandate ignores situational needs.

distractors.text: Delete the existing lessons-learned repository and start fresh with new project content.
distractors.trap: Discarding the knowledge throws away a valuable organizational asset.

explanation: PMBOK Guide 8 emphasizes that organizational process assets including lessons-learned databases must be used at moments of relevance to be useful. Process integration (C) creates that 'right moment' touchpoint.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.2 OPAs
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 7

prompts: Which of the following are signs that a project manager is effectively empowering their team? (Choose 3)

correct.text: Team members feel safe raising disagreements with the PM.
correct.why: Empowered teams show psychological safety, autonomous execution, and proactive problem ownership (Agile Practice Guide, PMBOK Guide 8).

correct.text: The team makes most day-to-day execution decisions without escalating.
correct.why: Empowered teams show psychological safety, autonomous execution, and proactive problem ownership (Agile Practice Guide, PMBOK Guide 8).

correct.text: Team members proactively flag impediments and proposed solutions.
correct.why: Empowered teams show psychological safety, autonomous execution, and proactive problem ownership (Agile Practice Guide, PMBOK Guide 8).

distractors.text: The PM approves every technical choice before implementation.
distractors.trap: Micromanaging undermines team autonomy and trust.

distractors.text: All communication with stakeholders flows through the PM only.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — team members feel safe raising disagreements with the PM — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: Empowered teams show psychological safety, autonomous execution, and proactive problem ownership (Agile Practice Guide, PMBOK Guide 8). Approval gating (C) and communication funneling (E) are disempowerment markers.
references: Agile Practice Guide §4
PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §3.6 'Be an Accountable Leader'
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 3

prompts: On a large predictive project, the project manager has team members at very different experience levels. Which leadership approach is MOST appropriate?

correct.text: Situational — directing, coaching, supporting, delegating — to each member's readiness.
correct.why: Situational leadership tailors style to readiness on each task.

distractors.text: Directive — apply a uniform telling style across all team members for consistency throughout the project's execution phase.
distractors.trap: A rigid, one-size-fits-all mandate ignores situational needs.

distractors.text: Round robin — rotate the leadership role weekly across team members regardless of their competence.
distractors.trap: A rigid, one-size-fits-all mandate ignores situational needs.

distractors.text: Decentral — delegate all project decisions to senior members and exclude juniors from discussions.
distractors.trap: Over-delegating abdicates the PM's accountability.

explanation: ECO Task 3 enabler 'Determine an appropriate leadership style.' Situational leadership tailors style to readiness on each task. Uniformity (A) underserves juniors or stifles seniors; rotation (B) ignores competence; exclusion (C) blocks development.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §3.6
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 3

prompts: An agile team delivers usable increments every two weeks but a key business stakeholder consistently skips reviews and then raises concerns weeks later. What should the project manager do?

correct.text: Analyze the stakeholder's needs and tailor engagement so they can engage meaningfully.
correct.why: ECO Task 4 enabler 'Analyze and tailor communication to stakeholder needs.'

distractors.text: Send all increments to the stakeholder by email and stop holding review sessions.
distractors.trap: Removing a valuable feedback ceremony loses inspect-and-adapt benefits.

distractors.text: Remove the stakeholder from the project's stakeholder register since they are unengaged.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — analyze the stakeholder's needs and tailor engagement so they can engage… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Continue without the stakeholder; absence from reviews implies tacit approval.
distractors.trap: Assuming rather than verifying introduces avoidable risk.

explanation: ECO Task 4 enabler 'Analyze and tailor communication to stakeholder needs.' The Agile Practice Guide notes engagement formats must fit the stakeholder's reality. Assuming consent (A), removal (C), and abandoning the review (D) all degrade inspect-and-adapt.
references: Agile Practice Guide §5 stakeholder engagement
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 4

prompts: Which of the following are effective practices for promoting a shared product vision to a distributed agile team? (Choose 3)

correct.text: Display the vision and product goal prominently in the team's shared workspace.
correct.why: Agile Practice Guide emphasizes transparency of vision across the whole team.

correct.text: Reference the vision when discussing trade-offs during backlog refinement.
correct.why: Visible artifacts, trade-off conversations, and recurring touchpoints at planning keep the vision current.

correct.text: Open each iteration planning event with a brief review of the vision.
correct.why: Visible artifacts, trade-off conversations, and recurring touchpoints at planning keep the vision current.

distractors.text: Limit vision discussions to the product owner only.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — display the vision and product goal prominently in the team's shared… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Replace the vision with iteration goals once development starts.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — display the vision and product goal prominently in the team's shared… — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: Agile Practice Guide emphasizes transparency of vision across the whole team. Visible artifacts, trade-off conversations, and recurring touchpoints at planning keep the vision current. Limiting to the PO (C) breaks transparency; replacing vision with iteration goals (E) conflates long-term direction with short-term scope.
references: Agile Practice Guide §3, §5
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 1

prompts: During a sprint review, a senior business stakeholder publicly criticizes a developer's implementation choices in front of the whole team. The developer becomes defensive. What should the project manager / Scrum Master do?

correct.text: Redirect feedback to the increment rather than the person, and follow up privately.
correct.why: Refocusing the feedback on the work and handling the interpersonal piece privately addresses both immediate dynamics and underlying issue.

distractors.text: End the current sprint review session and reschedule it for a later date.
distractors.trap: The Sprint Review inspects the increment, not individual performance (Scrum Guide).

distractors.text: Ask the product owner to publicly defend the developer's implementation choices.
distractors.trap: Handling feedback publicly embarrasses people and harms trust.

distractors.text: Allow the exchange to continue so the developer learns to handle stakeholder criticism.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — redirect feedback to the increment rather than the person — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: The Sprint Review inspects the increment, not individual performance (Scrum Guide). Refocusing the feedback on the work and handling the interpersonal piece privately addresses both immediate dynamics and underlying issue. (B) risks team safety; (C) overcorrects; (D) makes the PO defensive.
references: Scrum Guide 'Sprint Review'
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 2

prompts: Two stakeholders submit contradictory change requests in the same change control board meeting. Which is the MOST appropriate first action?

correct.text: Facilitate discussion of the underlying interests behind each request before the CCB decides.
correct.why: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders Performance Domain): conflicts often arise from differing underlying interests; surfacing them allows integrative solutions.

distractors.text: Defer both change requests to the next CCB cycle without further comment or action since this approach is straightforward.
distractors.trap: Mishandles the requests by skipping individual impact analysis.

distractors.text: Reject both change requests and ask the stakeholders to reconcile and resubmit them.
distractors.trap: Mishandles the requests by skipping individual impact analysis.

distractors.text: Approve both change requests and let the execution phase resolve the contradiction.
distractors.trap: Mishandles the requests by skipping individual impact analysis.

explanation: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders Performance Domain): conflicts often arise from differing underlying interests; surfacing them allows integrative solutions. (A) creates downstream chaos; (C) abdicates the PM's facilitation role; (D) delays without addressing the conflict.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.5 Stakeholders Performance Domain
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 2

prompts: A team is geographically distributed across three time zones. Synchronous meetings are difficult to schedule. What is the BEST approach?

correct.text: Combine minimum synchronous touchpoints with strong asynchronous practices for the rest.
correct.why: The Agile Practice Guide notes distributed teams require deliberate communication design.

distractors.text: Cancel all of the team's meetings to remove the time-zone scheduling problem entirely.
distractors.trap: Cancelling is a disproportionate overreaction to the situation.

distractors.text: Have only one time zone of the team work on the project at any given iteration cycle.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — combine minimum synchronous touchpoints with strong asynchronous practices for the rest — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Insist on holding synchronous meetings at hours that are convenient to the largest team group.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — combine minimum synchronous touchpoints with strong asynchronous practices for the rest — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: The Agile Practice Guide notes distributed teams require deliberate communication design. PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders): tailor channel and timing. The mix balances cohesion with respect for team members' working hours.
references: Agile Practice Guide §6 distributed teams
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 8

prompts: A team's daily standup has drifted into a status report to the project manager. What is the BEST corrective action?

correct.text: Refocus the event on coordination and impediments; the PM may attend as an observer only.
correct.why: The Agile Practice Guide and Scrum Guide both describe the Daily Scrum/standup as the development team's planning and coordination event, not a status report.

distractors.text: Cancel the daily standup, since it has drifted away from its original intended purpose to keep the schedule predictable.
distractors.trap: The Agile Practice Guide and Scrum Guide both describe the Daily Scrum/standup as the development team's planning and coordination event, not a status report.

distractors.text: Lengthen the daily standup so it can cover a wider set of topics than just coordination.
distractors.trap: The Agile Practice Guide and Scrum Guide both describe the Daily Scrum/standup as the development team's planning and coordination event, not a status report.

distractors.text: Continue with the current style; this gives the project manager useful daily information.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — refocus the event on coordination and impediments; the PM may attend… — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: The Agile Practice Guide and Scrum Guide both describe the Daily Scrum/standup as the development team's planning and coordination event, not a status report. Refocusing maintains its purpose.
references: Scrum Guide 'Daily Scrum'
Agile Practice Guide §5
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 8

prompts: Customer satisfaction surveys mid-project show a declining trend despite on-time, on-budget delivery against the baseline. What should the project manager do FIRST?

correct.text: Investigate the gap between baseline conformance and satisfaction to find expectation drift.
correct.why: PMBOK Guide 8: customer satisfaction is a value indicator distinct from baseline conformance.

distractors.text: Reduce the project's communication frequency to avoid drawing more attention to the gap.
distractors.trap: Avoidance defers the problem rather than resolving it.

distractors.text: Increase the volume of marketing of project successes directly to the customer organization.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — investigate the gap between baseline conformance and satisfaction to find expectation… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Ignore the satisfaction surveys; baseline performance is the contractual measure used as the most direct path forward.
distractors.trap: Ignoring the issue lets it grow instead of addressing it.

explanation: ECO Task 6 enabler 'Monitor internal and external customer satisfaction/expectations and respond as needed.' PMBOK Guide 8: customer satisfaction is a value indicator distinct from baseline conformance. Investigating expectation drift is the actionable step.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §3.4 'Focus on Value'
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 6

prompts: An agile team running two-week iterations has held a retrospective at the end of each one, but the project manager has noticed the team keeps re-discovering the same lessons rather than carrying them forward. An agile team's retrospectives generate insights every iteration, but lessons rarely persist beyond a couple of iterations and frequently get re-learned. What is the BEST corrective action?

correct.text: Document key outputs in a shared location and fold actions into working agreements or DoD.
correct.why: Persisting insights in living artifacts (working agreements, DoD) embeds learning.

distractors.text: Reduce the team's retrospective frequency from each iteration to once per release boundary.
distractors.trap: Removing a valuable feedback ceremony loses inspect-and-adapt benefits.

distractors.text: Stop running team retrospectives, since the insights they produce are not being retained.
distractors.trap: Removing a valuable feedback ceremony loses inspect-and-adapt benefits.

distractors.text: Have only senior members of the team attend the retrospectives going forward each iteration.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — document key outputs in a shared location and fold actions into… — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: Agile Practice Guide: retrospectives drive continuous improvement only when actions are followed through. Persisting insights in living artifacts (working agreements, DoD) embeds learning.
references: Agile Practice Guide §5 retrospectives
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 7

prompts: Over the last three months on a multi-team hybrid program, the same kinds of disputes — about handoffs, ownership of integration work, and prioritization — keep coming back. A project manager observes that conflicts in their hybrid project keep recurring on the same topics. What is the MOST productive response?

correct.text: Analyze the patterns to find structural causes — unclear roles, scarce resources, competing aims.
correct.why: Recurring conflicts almost always signal a structural issue (roles, priorities, resources) rather than personality.

distractors.text: Treat the recurrence as evidence of team dysfunction and request team-member changes.
distractors.trap: Blaming people damages trust and ignores systemic causes.

distractors.text: Introduce a no-conflict rule for the upcoming phase to maintain project momentum.
distractors.trap: Suppressing conflict hides problems instead of resolving them.

distractors.text: Hire an external mediator for every dispute that arises within the project team to maintain process consistency.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — analyze the patterns to find structural causes — unclear roles — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: ECO Task 2 enabler 'Identify conflict sources.' Recurring conflicts almost always signal a structural issue (roles, priorities, resources) rather than personality. PMBOK Guide 8's 'Be an Accountable Leader' calls for addressing the system. Suppression (C) hides the signal; external mediation (D) is expensive overkill before diagnosis.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §3.6
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 2

prompts: Which artifact BEST documents who needs what information, when, and how on a predictive project?

correct.text: The communications management plan, which defines audience, content, format, and timing.
correct.why: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders Performance Domain): the communications management plan defines the information needs, format, frequency, and channels for each audience.

distractors.text: The project quality management plan, which defines quality criteria and review processes.
distractors.trap: A real project artifact, but not the one that serves this question's purpose.

distractors.text: The stakeholder register alone, which lists each stakeholder and their interest level.
distractors.trap: A real project artifact, but not the one that serves this question's purpose.

distractors.text: The project risk register, which lists threats and opportunities identified by the team.
distractors.trap: A real project artifact, but not the one that serves this question's purpose.

explanation: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders Performance Domain): the communications management plan defines the information needs, format, frequency, and channels for each audience.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.5
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 8

prompts: Several stakeholders expect agile delivery to mean 'we can change anything at any time at no cost.' How should the project manager align expectations?

correct.text: Explain that agile welcomes change but new work displaces other work in the same iteration.
correct.why: The Agile Practice Guide is explicit that responding to change does not mean 'free' change — capacity is finite; new work displaces other work.

distractors.text: Refuse to discuss agile principles with stakeholders who are not familiar with agile.
distractors.trap: Declining sidesteps a responsibility the PM should own.

distractors.text: Convert the project back to a predictive approach to better manage scope expectations.
distractors.trap: Reverting the whole approach overreacts instead of fixing the issue.

distractors.text: Agree, since agile values responding to change over rigidly following a written plan while preserving the project's governance.
distractors.trap: The Agile Practice Guide is explicit that responding to change does not mean 'free' change — capacity is finite; new work displaces other work.

explanation: The Agile Practice Guide is explicit that responding to change does not mean 'free' change — capacity is finite; new work displaces other work. Honest framing aligns expectations without abandoning agility.

distractors.trap: The team owns its working agreements; addressing the violation reinforces both the norm and team ownership (Agile Practice Guide).

distractors.trap: The team owns its working agreements; addressing the violation reinforces both the norm and team ownership (Agile Practice Guide).

The team owns its working agreements; addressing the violation reinforces both the norm and team ownership (Agile Practice Guide). Replacement (A) is disproportionate; loosening (B) signals norms are negotiable; ignoring (D) erodes safety.

prompts: A heavily regulated project in financial services is being initiated, and the project manager is concerned about overlooking key stakeholders whose interests could shape the project's outcome. distractors.text: Ignore the regulator until they initiate contact with the project team themselves.
distractors.trap: Ignoring the issue lets it grow instead of addressing it.

distractors.text: Manage the regulator closely with constant project updates and frequent meetings.
distractors.trap: Constant updates (B) waste effort; ignoring (A) risks late escalation; over-engaging (D) misallocates attention.

distractors.text: Treat the regulator as if both their power and current interest level were high.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — keep informed at an appropriate level — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders Performance Domain) describes the power/interest grid: high power / low interest stakeholders should be kept satisfied with appropriate information. Constant updates (B) waste effort; ignoring (A) risks late escalation; over-engaging (D) misallocates attention.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.5 Stakeholders Performance Domain
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 4

prompts: Trust between an agile team and a critical customer stakeholder is low after a missed commitment last quarter. What is the MOST effective way to rebuild trust?

correct.text: Deliver small working increments frequently and inspect outcomes with the stakeholder.
correct.why: Working increments at a steady cadence give direct evidence.

distractors.text: Replace members of the team that was responsible for the original missed commitment.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — deliver small working increments frequently and inspect outcomes with the stakeholder — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Commit to a longer multi-year roadmap to demonstrate the team's seriousness to maintain process consistency.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — deliver small working increments frequently and inspect outcomes with the stakeholder — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Stop sharing progress reports until reliability has been restored within the team.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — deliver small working increments frequently and inspect outcomes with the stakeholder — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: The Agile Practice Guide: trust is built through transparency and demonstrated delivery, not promises. Working increments at a steady cadence give direct evidence. Bigger promises (B) repeat the antipattern; silence (C) deepens distrust; team replacement (A) doesn't address the engagement pattern.
references: Agile Practice Guide §3, §5
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 4

prompts: A team member with critical, undocumented knowledge of a legacy system is preparing to leave the project. What should the project manager do?

correct.text: Identify the critical knowledge, run transfer sessions, and capture key items before departure.
correct.why: ECO Task 7: 'Identify knowledge critical to the project,' 'Gather knowledge,' 'Foster an environment for knowledge transfer.'

distractors.text: Ask the team member to write a complete manual of their knowledge on their last day.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — identify the critical knowledge — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Accept the loss of the specialist's knowledge and hire a replacement after departure.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — identify the critical knowledge — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Defer all knowledge transfer activities until the replacement person has been hired.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — identify the critical knowledge — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: ECO Task 7: 'Identify knowledge critical to the project,' 'Gather knowledge,' 'Foster an environment for knowledge transfer.' PMBOK Guide 8 (Knowledge management) emphasizes both explicit and tacit knowledge transfer in advance.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.6 Resources Performance Domain
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 7

prompts: A functional manager who owns a small pool of senior engineers and the project manager have been deadlocked for two weeks about how much of one particular engineer's time the project gets, and both are now considering escalating to their respective executives. A functional manager and the project manager are in conflict over allocation of a critical engineer. Both are escalating to their executives. Which conflict-resolution approach should the project manager attempt FIRST?

correct.text: Collaborate to find a solution addressing both the project's and the manager's needs.
correct.why: PMBOK Guide 8 (Resources Performance Domain) and traditional conflict-management literature treat collaborate/problem-solve as the highest-quality resolution where time permits, producing win-win outcomes.

distractors.text: Force the issue, leveraging the project's higher organizational priority level.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — collaborate to find a solution addressing both the project's and the… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Smooth over the immediate disagreement and re-raise it again at a later date.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — collaborate to find a solution addressing both the project's and the… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Withdraw from the dispute and let the executives decide on resource allocation.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — collaborate to find a solution addressing both the project's and the… — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: PMBOK Guide 8 (Resources Performance Domain) and traditional conflict-management literature treat collaborate/problem-solve as the highest-quality resolution where time permits, producing win-win outcomes. Withdrawal (C) and smoothing (B) defer the problem; forcing (A) damages the relationship needed for future negotiations.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.6 Resources Performance Domain
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 2

prompts: An agile team co-located in a single room has been keeping its task board, burn-down chart, and working agreements in a private shared drive that only team members can open. An agile team's information radiators (task boards, burn-down charts, working agreements) are kept in a private team folder. The team is co-located. What is the BEST recommendation?

correct.text: Move information radiators to a visible space accessible to stakeholders who need them.
correct.why: The Agile Practice Guide explicitly endorses information radiators as a transparency mechanism — their value depends on visibility.

distractors.text: Keep radiators private to avoid stakeholder over-reaction to in-progress, unfinished data.
distractors.trap: Avoidance defers the problem rather than resolving it.

distractors.text: Update the team's information radiators only at the very end of each iteration cycle.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — move information radiators to a visible space accessible to stakeholders who… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Replace the existing information radiators with weekly written status reports for stakeholders.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — move information radiators to a visible space accessible to stakeholders who… — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: The Agile Practice Guide explicitly endorses information radiators as a transparency mechanism — their value depends on visibility. Hiding them defeats the purpose.
references: Agile Practice Guide §5 information radiators
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 8

prompts: A project manager interviews team members and finds that each person's understanding of project success differs significantly from the documented charter. What does this MOST likely indicate?

correct.text: Documented but not effectively communicated and internalized by the team.
correct.why: Documenting a vision is necessary but not sufficient.

distractors.text: The charter was incorrectly written and needs to be re-drafted from scratch.
distractors.trap: A real project artifact, but not the one that serves this question's purpose.

distractors.text: Team members are not paying enough attention to the available project documents.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — documented but not effectively communicated and internalized by the team — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: The project should be cancelled because the team is not aligned on its purpose.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — documented but not effectively communicated and internalized by the team — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: Documenting a vision is necessary but not sufficient. The ECO Task 1 enabler 'Promote the shared vision' explicitly distinguishes documentation from internalization. PMBOK Guide 8 emphasizes ongoing communication of purpose, not a one-time announcement.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §3.3, Stakeholders Performance Domain
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 1

prompts: Which BEST describes the purpose of a stakeholder engagement assessment matrix?

correct.text: Compare current engagement levels with desired levels and plan actions to close gaps.
correct.why: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders Performance Domain): the engagement assessment matrix compares current (C) and desired (D) engagement and drives targeted action.

distractors.text: To track each project stakeholder's preferred meeting time and format preferences.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — compare current engagement levels with desired levels and plan actions to… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Rank project stakeholders by their financial contribution to the overall project budget.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — compare current engagement levels with desired levels and plan actions to… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Document each stakeholder's preferred communication channel and nothing further.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — compare current engagement levels with desired levels and plan actions to… — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders Performance Domain): the engagement assessment matrix compares current (C) and desired (D) engagement and drives targeted action.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.5 Stakeholders Performance Domain
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 4

prompts: Two key stakeholders have stated expectations that, on closer reading, are contradictory. The project manager has not yet addressed this. What should they do?

correct.text: Facilitate a structured discussion to surface the contradiction and align expectations.
correct.why: ECO Task 5: 'Identify stakeholder expectations' and 'Facilitate discussions to align expectations.'

distractors.text: Document both expectations and let the execution phase reveal which one is feasible.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — facilitate a structured discussion to surface the contradiction and align expectations — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Adopt the expectation from the higher-ranking of the two stakeholders and proceed.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — facilitate a structured discussion to surface the contradiction and align expectations — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Average the two stakeholder expectations together into a single compromise expectation.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — facilitate a structured discussion to surface the contradiction and align expectations — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: ECO Task 5: 'Identify stakeholder expectations' and 'Facilitate discussions to align expectations.' PMBOK Guide 8 emphasizes early surfacing of conflicting expectations. Hierarchy-based picking damages the losing stakeholder; deferring wastes work; averaging produces an expectation neither owns.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.5
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 5

prompts: An agile product owner is the team's interface with most external stakeholders and is becoming a bottleneck. What is the BEST corrective action?

correct.text: Enable direct team-stakeholder clarifications, with the PO keeping backlog ordering authority.
correct.why: The Agile Practice Guide encourages direct conversation between developers and business stakeholders for clarification while preserving the product owner's accountability for backlog ordering.

distractors.text: Have the development team bypass the product owner on backlog decisions to move faster.
distractors.trap: Bypassing governance or compliance exposes the project to risk.

distractors.text: Replace the product owner with a different person who can handle the workload better.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — enable direct team-stakeholder clarifications — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Add a layer of business analysts between the product owner and the development team for the duration of the engagement.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — enable direct team-stakeholder clarifications — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: The Agile Practice Guide encourages direct conversation between developers and business stakeholders for clarification while preserving the product owner's accountability for backlog ordering. Replacing (A) is people-not-system; bypass (B) breaks accountability; extra layers (D) worsen the bottleneck.
references: Agile Practice Guide §4 roles
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 4

prompts: An external customer's procurement team and end-user team have very different expectations of the project. The PM has been talking primarily to procurement. What is the BEST course of action?

correct.text: Engage both groups, identify expectations, and align outcomes with end-user value.
correct.why: Engaging both groups aligns outcomes with the full customer picture.

distractors.text: Wait passively for the customer to raise the apparent misalignment between the groups.
distractors.trap: Passive waiting fails to take ownership of the issue.

distractors.text: Continue with procurement as the sole project interface; they were the contract signers.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — engage both groups, identify expectations, and align outcomes with end-user value — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Switch the sole project interface from procurement to the customer's end-user team.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — engage both groups, identify expectations, and align outcomes with end-user value — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: ECO Task 6 enabler 'Identify internal and external customer expectations.' PMBOK Guide 8 'Focus on Value': contractual conformance ≠ value to end users. Engaging both groups aligns outcomes with the full customer picture.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §3.4 'Focus on Value'
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 6

prompts: Two senior developers on an agile team have disagreed in three consecutive iterations about the team's branching strategy. Discussions in the retrospective have not resolved it and other team members are starting to take sides. What is the BEST action?

correct.text: Address it directly and privately, focusing on interests and the team's goals.
correct.why: ECO People Task 2 calls for addressing conflicts early, in private, with a collaborative approach.

distractors.text: Wait for the team to self-organize and reach consensus on the strategy for the duration of the engagement.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — address it directly and privately — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Move the disagreement out of retrospectives and into a written-only channel.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — address it directly and privately — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Decide on a branching strategy yourself and direct the team to follow it.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — address it directly and privately — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: ECO People Task 2 calls for addressing conflicts early, in private, with a collaborative approach. The Agile Practice Guide emphasizes servant leadership: surface the conflict's substance, then help parties find a resolution. Imposing a decision (A) bypasses ownership; passive waiting (B) lets factions harden; written-only channels (D) tend to escalate interpersonal conflict.
references: Agile Practice Guide §4 servant leadership
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 2

prompts: A project manager's status reports have become quite long. The sponsor has stopped reading them carefully. What is the BEST adjustment?

correct.text: Tailor length and content to the sponsor's actual decision needs, leading with key items.
correct.why: PMBOK Guide 8 'Adopt a Holistic View' includes attending to the audience's actual decision needs.

distractors.text: Stop sending the regular status reports to the sponsor since they are not being read.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — tailor length and content to the sponsor's actual decision needs — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Make the project's status report even longer to ensure no information at all is missed.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — tailor length and content to the sponsor's actual decision needs — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Continue with the same lengthy report format; the sponsor's reading habits are not the PM's concern.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — tailor length and content to the sponsor's actual decision needs — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: ECO Task 8 enabler 'Create reports aligned with sponsors and stakeholder expectations.' PMBOK Guide 8 'Adopt a Holistic View' includes attending to the audience's actual decision needs.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §3.3
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 8

prompts: A 14-month project is now within two weeks of formal closure and the project manager is planning what to capture. A project is closing. The project manager wants to ensure knowledge gained is preserved for future similar projects. Which action contributes MOST?

correct.text: Run final lessons sessions, capture successes and failures, and contribute to OPAs.
correct.why: ECO Task 7 and Process Task 10 (project closure): final lessons learned and updates to OPAs are explicit closure activities.

distractors.text: Delete the closing project's documents to free up storage on the organization's drives.
distractors.trap: Discarding the knowledge throws away a valuable organizational asset.

distractors.text: Wait six months and then write the lessons learned from memory after the team disbands.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — run final lessons sessions — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Send a one-page summary of the project's results to the executive sponsor only.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — run final lessons sessions — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: ECO Task 7 and Process Task 10 (project closure): final lessons learned and updates to OPAs are explicit closure activities. PMBOK Guide 8 emphasizes capturing learning while it is fresh and contributing it to the organizational repository.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §4.5.5 Closing Focus Area
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 7 , Process Task 10

prompts: A senior architect is preparing to leave the company within two months and the project manager is planning how to preserve their judgment-based knowledge. Tacit knowledge is BEST transferred through which method?

correct.text: Through direct conversation, pairing, mentoring, or shadowing alongside the practitioner.
correct.why: Tacit knowledge transfers primarily through interaction; documentation alone is insufficient.

distractors.text: Through recorded video lectures viewed individually by people who need the knowledge.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — through direct conversation, pairing, mentoring, or shadowing alongside the practitioner — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Through email distribution lists targeted at the relevant audience within the organization.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — through direct conversation, pairing, mentoring, or shadowing alongside the practitioner — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Through comprehensive written documentation that fully captures the practitioner's experience.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — through direct conversation, pairing, mentoring, or shadowing alongside the practitioner — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: PMBOK Guide 8 distinguishes tacit knowledge (skills, experience, judgment) from explicit knowledge (facts, procedures). Tacit knowledge transfers primarily through interaction; documentation alone is insufficient. The Agile Practice Guide endorses pairing for tacit transfer.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition knowledge management
Agile Practice Guide §4
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 7

prompts: Halfway through an agile product development, the product owner is replaced. The new product owner expresses a strategic direction that differs from what the team has been building. What is the BEST action?

correct.text: Convene a vision-alignment workshop with the team before the next planning event.
correct.why: A workshop refreshes the shared vision collaboratively.

distractors.text: Archive the current product backlog so the new product owner can rebuild it alone.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — convene a vision-alignment workshop with the team before the next planning… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Stop all development until executive sponsors choose between the two directions.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — convene a vision-alignment workshop with the team before the next planning… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Continue executing the current iteration backlog to preserve velocity.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — convene a vision-alignment workshop with the team before the next planning… — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: The Agile Practice Guide stresses keeping the product vision current as conditions change and treating it as a living artifact. A workshop refreshes the shared vision collaboratively. (A) ignores the strategic shift, (B) over-escalates, (D) excludes the team's domain knowledge.
references: Agile Practice Guide §3 vision
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 1

prompts: A stakeholder claims they were not informed about a key project decision that was clearly described in the last status report. What does this MOST likely indicate?

correct.text: Information was sent but not received and understood — the communication loop is incomplete.
correct.why: Communication is only complete when received and understood.

distractors.text: The stakeholder is being deliberately dishonest about whether they received the information.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — information was sent but not received and understood — the communication… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: The current status report format is fine; the stakeholder is at fault for missing the item.
distractors.trap: A real project artifact, but not the one that serves this question's purpose.

distractors.text: All status reports should be discontinued immediately to avoid this kind of dispute.
distractors.trap: Avoidance defers the problem rather than resolving it.

explanation: Communication is only complete when received and understood. ECO Task 8 enabler 'Establish a feedback loop.' PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders) recognizes that signal-out is not the same as signal-received; confirmation closes the loop.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.5
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 8

prompts: A newly assigned project manager takes over a 14-month digital transformation spanning three business units. Each unit's leadership describes the project's purpose in noticeably different terms. What should the project manager do FIRST?

correct.text: Run a facilitated session to converge on a shared vision and success criteria.
correct.why: ECO People Task 1 emphasizes helping ensure a shared vision with key stakeholders.

distractors.text: Document each unit's interpretation in the stakeholder register and proceed.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — run a facilitated session to converge on a shared vision and… — addresses the situation more effectively.

distractors.text: Adopt the sponsor's wording and apply it across the three business units in accordance with the existing plan.
distractors.trap: A facilitated convergence session addresses the root cause; copying the sponsor's wording (C) papers over misalignment, documenting and proceeding (B) preserves it, and escalation (D) is.

distractors.text: Escalate the discrepancy to the steering committee and pause project work.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — run a facilitated session to converge on a shared vision and… — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: ECO People Task 1 emphasizes helping ensure a shared vision with key stakeholders. PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders Performance Domain and the 'Adopt a Holistic View' principle) stresses that misalignment on purpose at the outset jeopardizes value delivery. A facilitated convergence session addresses the root cause; copying the sponsor's wording (C) papers over misalignment, documenting and proceeding (B) preserves it, and escalation (D) is premature.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §3.3 'Adopt a Holistic View'
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 1

prompts: A project manager is explaining communication design choices to a new team lead. Which characteristic distinguishes 'push' from 'pull' communication?

correct.text: Push delivers information whether the recipient asked for it; pull makes information available.
correct.why: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders Performance Domain) describes three communication modes: interactive (two-way), push (sent to recipients), and pull (made available for recipients to access).

distractors.text: Push is always verbal communication; pull is always written communication and documents.
distractors.trap: Relying on undocumented agreements creates ambiguity and risk.

distractors.text: There is no meaningful distinction between push and pull communication on a project.
distractors.trap: Rests on a false premise about how the approach actually works.

distractors.text: Push communication uses email channels; pull communication uses Slack or chat channels.
distractors.trap: The correct choice — push delivers information whether the recipient asked for it; pull makes… — addresses the situation more effectively.

explanation: PMBOK Guide 8 (Stakeholders Performance Domain) describes three communication modes: interactive (two-way), push (sent to recipients), and pull (made available for recipients to access). Information radiators are a pull mechanism.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition §2.5
Agile Practice Guide §5
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People Task 8

prompts: Stakeholders from different regions do not agree on the project objectives, and the disagreement starts creating confusion in scope decisions. What should the project manager have done to reduce the chance of this problem?

correct.text: Facilitate early discussions with the stakeholders to align objectives, assumptions, and expectations.
correct.why: Early, proactive facilitation aligns objectives and expectations before they fracture scope decisions.

distractors.text: Document the majority position in the charter and ask dissenting stakeholders to raise concerns during planning.
distractors.trap: Documenting a majority position sidelines dissenters instead of building genuine alignment.

distractors.text: Share a draft scope statement early and ask each regional lead to confirm whether it reflects local expectations.
distractors.trap: Sharing a draft for confirmation is reactive — it checks a document rather than facilitating real alignment.

distractors.text: Proceed with the majority view and address objections after planning is complete.
distractors.trap: Proceeding on the majority view lets misalignment harden and invites rework.

explanation: The problem is a failure of early alignment and engagement, not just disagreement. The PMI mindset favours proactive communication, expectation-setting, and collaborative stakeholder engagement before scope decisions are made.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People

prompts: A key stakeholder leaves the organization and delegates responsibility to a replacement. The replacement is not engaged and has not responded to recent project communications. What should the project manager do first?

correct.text: Schedule a short meeting with the new stakeholder to explain the project goals, understand their concerns, and assess their engagement level.
correct.why: Direct conversation rebuilds engagement and surfaces the new stakeholder's concerns first.

distractors.text: Ask the sponsor to reinforce with the new stakeholder that project participation is required.
distractors.trap: Escalating to the sponsor to compel participation skips understanding the stakeholder first.

distractors.text: Ask the stakeholder's direct reports which project details the new stakeholder is most concerned about before changing the engagement plan.
distractors.trap: Quizzing their direct reports gathers second-hand detail instead of engaging the stakeholder directly.

distractors.text: Update the stakeholder engagement assessment matrix and send the new stakeholder a revised communication schedule.
distractors.trap: Updating the matrix and sending a schedule is a documentation response to an engagement problem.

explanation: A disengaged stakeholder is an engagement problem, not a documentation problem. The best first step is direct communication to understand context and rebuild alignment.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People

prompts: Work is on track when a major accident occurs on site. What should the project manager do first?

correct.text: Ensure the safety of the people on site and secure the immediate area.
correct.why: Safety of people comes before schedule, cost, documentation, or investigation.

distractors.text: Notify site leadership and relevant emergency contacts according to the incident response procedure.
distractors.trap: Notifying leadership matters, but it comes after making people safe and securing the area.

distractors.text: Pause work in the affected area and begin collecting information needed for compliance reporting.
distractors.trap: Collecting compliance information is premature while people may still be in danger.

distractors.text: Inform stakeholders that the schedule and budget will be affected.
distractors.trap: Worrying about schedule and budget before safety inverts the priority.

explanation: Safety comes before schedule, cost, documentation, or investigation. Once people are safe and the situation is controlled, the PM can follow the incident, issue, and root-cause processes.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People

prompts: A team member who has usually performed well has recently started missing commitments after the project introduced a new collaboration tool. The functional manager asks the project manager to solve the problem quickly because delivery dates are at risk. What should the project manager do first?

correct.text: Meet privately with the team member to understand the cause and determine what support is needed.
correct.why: Understanding the root cause first lets the PM choose the right support (servant leadership).

distractors.text: Ask the functional manager to replace the team member with someone more experienced.
distractors.trap: Replacing the member reacts to a symptom without diagnosing the cause.

distractors.text: Arrange formal training on the new collaboration tool for the whole team.
distractors.trap: Team-wide training assumes a skill gap before the real obstacle is known.

distractors.text: Escalate the performance issue to the sponsor because deadlines are now at risk.
distractors.trap: Escalating to the sponsor over-reacts before understanding what changed for the individual.

explanation: Servant leadership and root-cause thinking call for first understanding whether the issue is skill, workload, motivation, or tool usability before choosing an intervention.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People

prompts: A project manager on an agile project makes most decisions without team input to save time, but team morale and engagement are declining. Which leadership approach would have been more appropriate?

correct.text: Servant leadership
correct.why: Servant leadership supports team autonomy by enabling collaboration, removing obstacles, and helping the team make effective decisions.

distractors.text: Command-and-control leadership
distractors.trap: Command-and-control may speed short-term decisions but undermines team ownership in agile environments

distractors.text: Top-down leadership
distractors.trap: Top-down leadership reduces shared accountability and conflicts with agile ways of working

distractors.text: Use of control accounts
distractors.trap: Control accounts are a work performance management tool, not a leadership behavior

explanation: Current PMI thinking emphasizes empowering teams, especially in agile and hybrid delivery. A project manager should create conditions for the team to succeed rather than making decisions for the team and then trying to gain agreement after the fact.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People

prompts: Two external consultants from different companies are repeatedly in conflict, and the disagreements are slowing progress on the project. Which stage of team development is often the hardest to move through in this situation?

correct.text: Storming from Forming, Storming, Norming, Performing
correct.why: Storming is the stage where conflict, role tension, and competing viewpoints commonly emerge as the team works toward alignment.

distractors.text: Panic from Enthusiasm, Panic, Hope, Solution
distractors.trap: This is not the standard team development model used in PMI guidance

distractors.text: Kickoff from Assignment, Kickoff, Training, Communicating
distractors.trap: These are not recognized stages of team development in this context

distractors.text: Frustration from Direction, Frustration, Cooperation, Collaboration
distractors.trap: This is not the accepted model for describing team development stages on the PMP exam

explanation: PMI commonly uses Tuckman's team development model to explain how teams mature. In the storming stage, disagreement is normal as people test boundaries, influence, and working styles, and unresolved conflict can stall progress if not managed well.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition
PMP Examination Content Outline 2026 - People

prompts: A project team is creating its approach for managing uncertainty on a project. What does the risk management plan describe?

correct.text: How risk management activities will be structured and carried out throughout the project
correct.why: The risk management plan defines the approach, roles, methods, and processes the team will use to manage risk.

distractors.text: The individual risks the team has already identified and assigned for action
distractors.trap: This describes content typically maintained in a risk register, not the management approach

distractors.text: The high-level risks that will be escalated to senior leadership for decisions
distractors.trap: This is only one possible subset of risk information, not the purpose of the plan

distractors.text: The sources of uncertainty that cannot be addressed through analysis or response planning
distractors.trap: This confuses uncertainty concepts with the purpose of the planning document

explanation: Under current PMI guidance, the risk management plan explains how the team will identify, analyze, respond to, monitor, and communicate risks. It is a governance and working approach document, not a list of specific risks. This remains valid across predictive, agile, and hybrid delivery.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition

prompts: A project manager decides to fast-track two phases that were originally planned to occur one after the other. One team of five people is already working, and another team of five was scheduled to start in four weeks. What should the project manager do first?

correct.text: Reorganize roles and responsibilities so the combined team can coordinate work effectively during the overlap
correct.why: Fast-tracking increases coordination complexity, so the first priority is to organize the team for effective collaboration and decision-making.

distractors.text: Confirm that the upcoming phase has met all exit criteria before increasing cross-team communication
distractors.trap: Exit criteria apply to the phase being completed, not the phase about to begin

distractors.text: Hold a phase review to verify that the earlier phase has met all entry criteria before technical work overlaps
distractors.trap: Entry criteria are not the main issue when intentionally overlapping work

distractors.text: Hold a phase gate to confirm that the earlier phase has fully met exit criteria before any overlap begins
distractors.trap: Fast-tracking begins before full completion, so waiting for all exit criteria defeats the purpose

explanation: In current PMI practice, fast-tracking can shorten the schedule but increases communication needs, dependencies, and rework risk. The project manager should first enable effective coordination by clarifying responsibilities, interfaces, and working agreements across the overlapping teams. That creates the conditions for controlled overlap rather than confusion.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition

prompts: A project team has outsourced part of the work to a service provider. The team wants to confirm that the provider’s quality practices are effective and aligned with the customer’s requirements and the project’s quality expectations. Which activity is most likely to provide that assurance?

correct.text: Conduct a quality audit
correct.why: A quality audit evaluates whether the provider’s quality processes are being followed and are effective.

distractors.text: Perform an inspection of completed deliverables
distractors.trap: An inspection checks outputs, not whether the underlying quality system is functioning effectively

distractors.text: Use a fixed-price contract
distractors.trap: A contract type does not verify quality performance or process effectiveness

distractors.text: Define a service level agreement
distractors.trap: A service level agreement sets expectations but does not confirm they are being met

explanation: In current PMI practice, audits are used to assess process compliance and identify improvement opportunities. If the concern is whether a contractor’s quality system is working as intended, reviewing the process is more appropriate than only checking final outputs.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition

prompts: Early in a project, you want to gather perspectives from subject matter experts in different disciplines to discuss requirements, product expectations, and potential risks in an informal, conversational setting. Which technique is most appropriate?

correct.text: Facilitate a focus group
correct.why: A focus group brings selected experts together to discuss perceptions, expectations, and concerns in an interactive setting.

distractors.text: Conduct a peer review
distractors.trap: Peer review evaluates work products rather than encouraging broad exploratory discussion

distractors.text: Perform a SWOT analysis
distractors.trap: SWOT is an analytical framework, not a facilitated group discussion technique for eliciting views

distractors.text: Calculate monetary value
distractors.trap: Monetary value calculation supports decision analysis, not collaborative requirements exploration

explanation: A focus group is useful when the goal is to surface different viewpoints through guided discussion. It helps the team explore needs, assumptions, and risks by enabling participants to react to one another’s ideas in real time.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition

prompts: An organization is experiencing employee resistance during multiple change initiatives. A change expert recommends using the ADKAR model. What does this model help leaders understand?

correct.text: The five stages individuals move through to accept and support change
correct.why: ADKAR explains the individual change journey through five elements that help people adopt and sustain change.

distractors.text: Why most change initiatives fail and how project managers can reverse that trend
distractors.trap: ADKAR is not a statistical model explaining overall project failure rates

distractors.text: The planning domains management should complete before launching the change
distractors.trap: ADKAR does not define management planning domains for change initiatives

distractors.text: The hierarchy of needs employees expect to have met before supporting the work
distractors.trap: This describes a motivation theory, not the ADKAR model

explanation: ADKAR focuses on individual adoption of change by addressing awareness, desire, knowledge, ability, and reinforcement. In PMI-aligned change leadership, understanding how people progress through change helps the project manager and sponsors reduce resistance and increase adoption.

prompts: You are leading a project with a globally distributed virtual team. Colocation is not feasible, and progress is slowing because communication among team members is inefficient. Which action is most likely to help the team operate more cohesively and improve speed?

correct.text: Create a fishbowl window for quick, simple communication
correct.why: A fishbowl window enables fast, lightweight communication that helps virtual team members stay aligned and collaborate as one team.

distractors.text: Provide technical training to all team members
distractors.trap: Technical training does not directly address the communication friction slowing the team

distractors.text: Schedule daily phone calls and require detailed status reporting
distractors.trap: More meetings and reporting can add overhead rather than improve collaboration

distractors.text: Bring the team together for a meeting at a location convenient to everyone
distractors.trap: Colocation is stated to be impractical, so a physical meeting is not the most feasible response

explanation: For virtual teams, communication should be timely, accessible, and easy to use in day-to-day work. A simple shared communication channel can reduce delays, improve transparency, and strengthen team cohesion without creating unnecessary process burden.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition
